mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS exceeds +0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of high-rate observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5%, it signals labor market slack that reduces consumer spending power and corporate profit growth, triggering negative equity returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand expansion, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform baseline (Sharpe < 0.5) or yields negative returns in >55% of co-expansion periods over a 5-year evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid month-over-month rebound in the industrial production index following a contraction signals supply-side recovery, reducing input cost pressures and boosting equity valuations over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of recovery-phase observations, or the mechanism shows no statistical edge (p-value > 0.10) in regime-filtered backtests.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds a 3.5% year-over-year growth rate, inflation expectations force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and generating negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally (positive 20d returns > 65%) during high-inflation periods, or the threshold signal produces a negative Rank IC over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a stable or declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor demand without slack, supporting wage growth and consumer spending that drives positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative 60-day returns in >55% of triggered cases, or interaction effects vanish (coefficient insignificant) in multivariate regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth directly correlates with aggregate corporate earnings growth, providing a persistent positive signal for S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns are negative in >60% of positive-GDP-growth quarters.

mechanism_narrative: In a stagflationary environment, declining industrial production alongside rising inflation indicates supply constraints that erode profit margins, leading to negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in >60% of stagflationary observations, or the interaction term loses predictive significance (p > 0.05) in regime-specific models.
